info.name: Diksuchi AI Router — v3
info.description:
Complete API test collection for Diksuchi — Local-First AI Router.

Base URL: {{baseUrl}}
  Dev mode (make dev):   http://localhost:8081
  Docker mode (make up): http://localhost:8080

Folder order = recommended testing sequence:
  1. Health & Connectivity
  2. Routing Preview (dry-run)
  3. Chat Completions
  4. Provider Management
  5. Preferences (model pins + allowed-tiers)
  6. Ratings
  7. Stats & Logs
  8. Centroid Classifier (all 5 buckets)
  9. Error Cases

item.name: 🟢 1 — Health & Connectivity
item.description: Basic liveness checks. All should return 200 when the server is running.

item.name: 🔀 2 — Routing Preview (dry-run)
item.description:
POST /v1/route/preview — routing decisions without hitting any LLM.
Response: backend, provider, rule_name, reason, token_count, model_requested, prompt_preview, bucket, bucket_confidence

item.name: Preview — Force local via X-Route-To header
Preview — Force cloud via X-Route-To header

exec: pm.test('bucket is valid name if centroid fired', () => {

item.name: 💬 3 — Chat Completions
item.description:
35 test cases covering every routing path:
• Routing rules: headers, model hints, keywords, regex patterns, token count
• Parameters: temperature, max_tokens, system messages, multi-turn
• x_router assertions: backend, rule, model_used, bucket, fallback
• Response envelope: id, object, created, choices, usage, finish_reason
• Edge cases: unicode, JSON output, empty messages, nested content

request.description: Short, simple prompt → 'one sentence' keyword triggers simple_keywords_local → local.
System message tests correct extraction before sending to local/cloud.
3-turn thread — verifies the router passes full history upstream.
temperature=0 → most deterministic answer. Router must pass param through.
temperature=1.0 — ensures parameter is forwarded and response still returns.
max_tokens=20 caps the completion. Verifies the param reaches the provider.
Complex prompt that would normally go cloud — header forces local anyway.
Trivial prompt that would normally stay local — header forces cloud.
'architecture' is in complexity_keywords_cloud → cloud even without header.
'security audit' and 'comprehensive' both fire complexity_keywords_cloud.
'what is' fires simple_keywords_local → local.
'translate' in simple_keywords_local → local.
Traceback pattern triggers stack_trace_cloud regex rule → cloud.
'diff --git' pattern triggers git_diff_local regex rule → local.

item.name: 🏠 Token rule: short prompt (≤300 tokens) → local

request.description: Single-word prompt → well under 300 tokens → short_prompt_local rule fires.

item.name: ☁️ Token rule: long prompt (≥800 tokens) → cloud

request.description: Repeated text padded to 800+ tokens → long_prompt_cloud rule fires.
model=gpt-4o-mini → model_hint_cloud rule → cloud, ignores prompt simplicity.
model=llama3.2 → model_hint_local rule → local, ignores prompt complexity.
deepseek/ prefix → model_hint_cloud → cloud via openrouter_free.
Coding prompt → centroid:coding fires → x_router.bucket='coding', bucket_confidence ∈ (0,1).

exec: pm.test('x_router has bucket when centroid fired', () => {

request.description: Simple prompt → local succeeds first try → x_router.fallback must be false.
Multi-script Unicode + emoji — verifies no encoding issues in the proxy layer.
JSON-output prompt — tests that the proxy passes system instruction correctly.

exec: catch(e) { pm.expect.fail('Response is not valid JSON: ' + content.slice(0,100)); }

request.description: 'what does' keyword → local. Tests code block passthrough in message body.
'refactor this' is in complexity_keywords_cloud → cloud.
'add type hints' fires simple_keywords_local → local.

exec: pm.test('Routes to local (add type hints keyword)', () => {

request.description: api_key = 'sk-...' matches credentials_in_prompt_cloud regex → cloud (never cache locally).

item.name: 📊 Usage object — prompt + completion + total tokens

request.description: Verifies usage.total_tokens = prompt_tokens + completion_tokens (Ollama + cloud both return this).

exec: pm.test('Has usage object with token counts', () => {

request.description: Normal completion → finish_reason must be 'stop', not null or 'length'.
Verifies the OpenAI envelope shape: id, object, created, choices[0].message.
'code review' fires complexity_keywords_cloud → cloud.
Explicit gemma3:1b → model_hint_local → model_used must be gemma3:1b exactly.
No messages field → 422 Unprocessable Entity from FastAPI.
[] messages — provider-dependent: some return 400, some return empty completion.
content as array-of-parts (vision/multimodal format) — router must handle this for centroid classification.

item.name: ⚙️ 4 — Provider Management
item.description:
GET /v1/providers response shape:
  { local: {active, providers:[{name,type,base_url,default_model,models,model_count,enabled,active,side,has_key,api_key_env}]}, cloud: {...}, discovery: {last_run,next_run_in_h,counts} }

request.description: Returns 400 if OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set in .env
Restores previous active_cloud and fallback_chain from config.yaml

item.name: 🎯 5 — Preferences
item.description:
Per-bucket model pins + allowed-tiers routing policy.
Buckets: simple | coding | reasoning | creative | critical
Tiers: 0=local | 1=openrouter_free | 2=paid cloud

exec: pm.test('allowed_tiers values are arrays of valid ints', () => {

item.name: POST — reset pin via empty string

request.description: Empty string = reset to auto-select

item.name: POST — allowed_tiers: skip local for reasoning [1,2]

request.description: Simple tasks always stay local — never spend cloud quota on them.

item.name: POST — allowed_tiers: reset all to default [0,1,2]

request.description: Server removes [0,1,2] overrides from prefs file to keep it clean

item.name: ⭐ 6 — Ratings
item.description: Model quality scores: HF LB v2 (0.55) + Bundled baseline (0.35) + Self-benchmark (0.10) + OR catalog (gap-fill)

request.description: Fetches from HF LB v2 + OR catalog + bundled baseline. Takes 10-60s.
Unknown bucket → 400

item.name: 📊 7 — Stats & Logs
item.description: Runtime counters and routing decision audit log.

request.description: Returns {entries:[...]}. Each entry: id, ts, backend, provider, rule, reason, tokens, model_requested, model_used, bucket, bucket_confidence, preview, duration_ms, status, error, fallback.

item.name: 🧠 8 — Centroid Classifier (all 5 buckets)
item.description:
One route/preview per bucket — confirms the centroid classifier (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) is working.
If confidence < 0.35, fires centroid:low_confidence → local.

request.description: Factual lookup → simple bucket → Tier 0 (local).
Implementation task → coding bucket.
Multi-step cognitive bias problem → reasoning bucket → cloud.
Stylistic creative writing → creative bucket.
Argument evaluation → critical bucket → highest tier.
Single word — confidence < 0.35 → centroid:low_confidence → local.
200 if OPENROUTER_API_KEY in .env, 400 if not. Both are valid.
